title: AI superintelligence, DOGE, and getting 'carried away' with politics: 5 key takeaways from Elon Musk's latest interview
excerpt: Elon Musk discussed AI surpassing human intelligence, political involvement, and AI lab policing during an interview with The Economist.
content_html:
Elon Musk sat down with The Economist's editor in chief Zanny Minton Beddoes on Thursday for a wide-ranging interview, covering everything from superintelligent AI to his controversial stint in government.

Musk, the world's richest person, is the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX and owns the social media platform X, where he has roughly 250 million followers. He also runs xAI, his artificial intelligence startup, putting him at the center of the AI race he discussed at length.

Here are the five biggest takeaways from the 90-minute interview:

1. Musk thinks AI will surpass the sum of human intelligence within roughly 5 years

Musk said AI may exceed humanity's collective intelligence "in around five years," adding that soon, "there really won't be anything that AI can't do better than humans, apart from being human, perhaps."

He painted a utopian picture of what comes next: an "age of amazing abundance" where "anyone can have anything they can think of."

However, he was less reassuring on who will be in charge, comparing the coming intelligence gap to the one between humans and chimpanzees: "It's hard to imagine that the chimpanzee would be in charge."

“There really won't be anything that AI can’t do better than humans, apart from being human, perhaps,” says @elonmusk.

He tells @zannymb that artificial intelligence may exceed the sum of human intelligence in about five years.

Tune in to The Insider from 6pm London time:… pic.twitter.com/Rnw3ngZy3F

— The Economist (@TheEconomist) July 23, 2026

2. He's made peace with an AI risk he can't stop

Musk has previously put the odds of AI wiping out humanity at 10% to 20%, and he didn't walk that back. What's changed is his attitude: he said he'd reached a "philosophical conclusion" to look on the bright side, because the momentum behind AI and robotics is now unstoppable.

"Let's enjoy the ride, is my philosophy at this point," he said, suggesting that even if a stop button existed, "we probably shouldn't press it."

He also said, with some irony, that his own attempts to steer AI safely, including co-founding OpenAI, which Anthropic later spun out of, mostly accelerated the technology. His conclusion: "You can't beat 'em? Join 'em."

3. He admits he got 'carried away' with politics

"I think I got a little too involved in politics, got carried away, frankly," Musk said, in one of the interview's more reflective moments.

Musk donated heavily to Trump's 2024 reelection campaign and took a prominent role in the early months of the administration, spearheading the Department of Government Efficiency. He later fell out with Trump over the president's Big Beautiful Bill.

In the interview, Musk defended the underlying aim of his DOGE work: tackling a deficit so large, he said, that interest payments on the debt now exceed US defense and intelligence spending combined.

The goal, he argued, was to make sure money wasn't being "wasted or spent on fraudulent activities," though he said he "got a lot of flak" for it.

4. He wants rival AI labs to police each other, despite the personal feuds

Musk proposed that leading AI companies hold safety calls every few weeks and review each other's new models before release, arguing competitors are better placed than governments to spot problems.

Minton Beddoes said she keeps hearing that a system needs to be in place within six months. Musk said that "six months is a long time," given the pace of breakthroughs.

Getting there would mean papering over some deep rifts. Musk repeated his grievance that OpenAI, which he cofounded as a nonprofit, became an $800 billion, closed-source, for-profit company. He also renewed his long-standing criticisms of fellow founder Sam Altman, with whom he has spent much of the past two years locked in a legal battle.

He praised Anthropic's Dario Amodei, who left OpenAI to found the company, as "a very principled person."

If it comes to it, he said, they'll "set aside our personal differences for the good of the world."

5. He continues to insist that 'zero' people died from USAID cuts

Musk flatly denied that the aid cuts presided over by his short-lived Department of Government Efficiency cost lives. Asked whether children in Africa died because funding was pulled so suddenly, he was emphatic: "Zero," later repeating "0.0"

He argued that philanthropic organizations like the Gates Foundation and MacKenzie Scott's Yield Giving could have stepped in — and if they didn't, "they're equally responsible" — and dismissed reports of deaths as being from "fraudulent organizations" seeking restored funding.

Minton Beddoes pushed back, saying the pace of the cuts "will almost certainly have caused unnecessary suffering and deaths."

Watch the full exchange here:

.@elonmusk says zero people died from DOGE cuts to USAID. The billionaire CEO of SpaceX and Tesla, in his own words, helped feed the policy to the woodchipper last year, when he was the head of America’s Department of Government Efficiency. In an exclusive interview, @zannymb,… pic.twitter.com/BilkbWrJHF

— The Economist (@TheEconomist) July 23, 2026

Read next

Georgia Hennessy

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Georgia is a fellow at Business Insider's London office.Before joining Business Insider, she worked at Japan's largest newspaper, The Yomiuri Shimbun, and interned at the Financial Times. She is an NCTJ-qualified journalist with a degree in Philosophy from the University of Birmingham. You can contact her via email at ghennessy@insider.com

sentences.zh: 奇普·索莫德维拉（Chip Somodevilla）来自盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: Elon Musk sat down with The Economist's editor in chief Zanny Minton Beddoes on Thursday for a wide-ranging interview, covering everything from superintelligent AI to his controversial stint in government.

sentences.en: Elon Musk sat down with The Economist's editor in chief Zanny Minton Beddoes on Thursday for a wide-ranging interview, covering everything from superintelligent AI to his controversial stint in government.
sentences.zh: 周四，埃隆·马斯克与《经济学人》主编赞尼·明顿·贝多斯 (Zanny Minton Beddoes) 进行了一次内容广泛的采访，采访内容涵盖了从超级人工智能到他在政府中备受争议的任期等方方面面。

blocks.html_en: Musk, the world's richest person, is the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX and owns the social media platform X, where he has roughly 250 million followers. He also runs xAI, his artificial intelligence startup, putting him at the center of the AI race he discussed at length.

sentences.en: Musk, the world's richest person, is the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX and owns the social media platform X, where he has roughly 250 million followers.
sentences.zh: 马斯克是世界首富，是特斯拉和 SpaceX 的首席执行官，并拥有社交媒体平台 X，在该平台上他拥有大约 2.5 亿粉丝。

sentences.en: He also runs xAI, his artificial intelligence startup, putting him at the center of the AI race he discussed at length.
sentences.zh: 他还经营着自己的人工智能初创公司 xAI，这使他处于他详细讨论的人工智能竞赛的中心。

blocks.html_en: Here are the five biggest takeaways from the 90-minute interview:

sentences.en: Here are the five biggest takeaways from the 90-minute interview:
sentences.zh: 以下是 90 分钟采访的五个最大收获：

blocks.html_en: 1. Musk thinks AI will surpass the sum of human intelligence within roughly 5 years

sentences.en: Musk thinks AI will surpass the sum of human intelligence within roughly 5 years
sentences.zh: 马斯克认为人工智能将在大约5年内超越人类智力的总和

blocks.html_en: Musk said AI may exceed humanity's collective intelligence "in around five years," adding that soon, "there really won't be anything that AI can't do better than humans, apart from being human, perhaps."

sentences.en: Musk said AI may exceed humanity's collective intelligence "in around five years," adding that soon, "there really won't be anything that AI can't do better than humans, apart from being human, perhaps."
sentences.zh: 马斯克表示，人工智能可能会在“大约五年内”超越人类的集体智慧，并补充说，很快，“除了人类之外，或许真的没有什么事情是人工智能不能比人类做得更好的了。”

blocks.html_en: He painted a utopian picture of what comes next: an "age of amazing abundance" where "anyone can have anything they can think of."

sentences.en: He painted a utopian picture of what comes next: an "age of amazing abundance" where "anyone can have anything they can think of."
sentences.zh: 他描绘了未来的乌托邦图景：一个“令人惊叹的丰富时代”，“任何人都可以拥有他们能想到的任何东西”。

blocks.html_en: However, he was less reassuring on who will be in charge, comparing the coming intelligence gap to the one between humans and chimpanzees: "It's hard to imagine that the chimpanzee would be in charge."

sentences.en: However, he was less reassuring on who will be in charge, comparing the coming intelligence gap to the one between humans and chimpanzees: "It's hard to imagine that the chimpanzee would be in charge."
sentences.zh: 然而，对于谁将负责，他不太放心，并将即将到来的智力差距与人类和黑猩猩之间的差距进行比较：“很难想象黑猩猩会负责。”

blocks.html_en: 2. He's made peace with an AI risk he can't stop

sentences.en: He's made peace with an AI risk he can't stop
sentences.zh: 他已经接受了无法阻止的人工智能风险

blocks.html_en: Musk has previously put the odds of AI wiping out humanity at 10% to 20%, and he didn't walk that back. What's changed is his attitude: he said he'd reached a "philosophical conclusion" to look on the bright side, because the momentum behind AI and robotics is now unstoppable.

sentences.en: Musk has previously put the odds of AI wiping out humanity at 10% to 20%, and he didn't walk that back.
sentences.zh: 马斯克此前曾将人工智能消灭人类的可能性定为 10% 到 20%，但他并没有改变这一说法。

sentences.en: What's changed is his attitude: he said he'd reached a "philosophical conclusion" to look on the bright side, because the momentum behind AI and robotics is now unstoppable.
sentences.zh: 改变的是他的态度：他说他已经得出了一个“哲学结论”，要看到光明的一面，因为人工智能和机器人背后的势头现在是不可阻挡的。

blocks.html_en: "Let's enjoy the ride, is my philosophy at this point," he said, suggesting that even if a stop button existed, "we probably shouldn't press it."

sentences.en: "Let's enjoy the ride, is my philosophy at this point," he said, suggesting that even if a stop button existed, "we probably shouldn't press it."
sentences.zh: “让我们享受这段旅程，这是我目前的理念，”他说，并暗示即使存在停止按钮，“我们可能也不应该按下它。”

blocks.html_en: He also said, with some irony, that his own attempts to steer AI safely, including co-founding OpenAI, which Anthropic later spun out of, mostly accelerated the technology. His conclusion: "You can't beat 'em? Join 'em."

sentences.en: He also said, with some irony, that his own attempts to steer AI safely, including co-founding OpenAI, which Anthropic later spun out of, mostly accelerated the technology.
sentences.zh: 他还讽刺地表示，他自己安全引导人工智能的尝试，包括共同创立 OpenAI（Anthropic 后来将其剥离出来），大部分都加速了这项技术的发展。

sentences.en: His conclusion: "You can't beat 'em?
sentences.zh: 他的结论是：“你不能打败他们吗？

sentences.en: Join 'em."
sentences.zh: 加入他们吧。”

blocks.html_en: 3. He admits he got 'carried away' with politics

sentences.en: He admits he got 'carried away' with politics
sentences.zh: 他承认自己对政治“着迷”

blocks.html_en: "I think I got a little too involved in politics, got carried away, frankly," Musk said, in one of the interview's more reflective moments.

sentences.en: "I think I got a little too involved in politics, got carried away, frankly," Musk said, in one of the interview's more reflective moments.
sentences.zh: “坦白说，我认为我对政治的参与有点太多了，有点得意忘形了，”马斯克在采访中一个更具反思性的时刻说道。

blocks.html_en: Musk donated heavily to Trump's 2024 reelection campaign and took a prominent role in the early months of the administration, spearheading the Department of Government Efficiency. He later fell out with Trump over the president's Big Beautiful Bill.

sentences.en: Musk donated heavily to Trump's 2024 reelection campaign and took a prominent role in the early months of the administration, spearheading the Department of Government Efficiency.
sentences.zh: 马斯克为特朗普 2024 年连任竞选活动提供了大量捐款，并在政府执政的最初几个月发挥了重要作用，领导了政府效率部。

sentences.en: He later fell out with Trump over the president's Big Beautiful Bill.
sentences.zh: 后来他因总统的“美丽大法案”而与特朗普闹翻。

blocks.html_en: In the interview, Musk defended the underlying aim of his DOGE work: tackling a deficit so large, he said, that interest payments on the debt now exceed US defense and intelligence spending combined.

sentences.en: In the interview, Musk defended the underlying aim of his DOGE work: tackling a deficit so large, he said, that interest payments on the debt now exceed US defense and intelligence spending combined.
sentences.zh: 在采访中，马斯克捍卫了他的 DOGE 工作的根本目标：解决巨额赤字，他说，债务利息支付现在超过了美国国防和情报支出的总和。

blocks.html_en: The goal, he argued, was to make sure money wasn't being "wasted or spent on fraudulent activities," though he said he "got a lot of flak" for it.

sentences.en: The goal, he argued, was to make sure money wasn't being "wasted or spent on fraudulent activities," though he said he "got a lot of flak" for it.
sentences.zh: 他认为，其目标是确保资金不会“浪费或用于欺诈活动”，尽管他表示自己因此“受到了很多批评”。

blocks.html_en: 4. He wants rival AI labs to police each other, despite the personal feuds

sentences.en: He wants rival AI labs to police each other, despite the personal feuds
sentences.zh: 他希望竞争对手的人工智能实验室能够互相监督，尽管存在私人恩怨

blocks.html_en: Musk proposed that leading AI companies hold safety calls every few weeks and review each other's new models before release, arguing competitors are better placed than governments to spot problems.

sentences.en: Musk proposed that leading AI companies hold safety calls every few weeks and review each other's new models before release, arguing competitors are better placed than governments to spot problems.
sentences.zh: 马斯克提议，领先的人工智能公司每隔几周举行一次安全电话会议，并在发布前审查彼此的新模型，他认为竞争对手比政府更有能力发现问题。

blocks.html_en: Minton Beddoes said she keeps hearing that a system needs to be in place within six months. Musk said that "six months is a long time," given the pace of breakthroughs.

sentences.en: Minton Beddoes said she keeps hearing that a system needs to be in place within six months.
sentences.zh: 明顿·贝多斯 (Minton Beddoes) 表示，她不断听说系统需要在六个月内到位。

sentences.en: Musk said that "six months is a long time," given the pace of breakthroughs.
sentences.zh: 马斯克表示，考虑到突破的速度，“六个月是一段很长的时间”。

blocks.html_en: Getting there would mean papering over some deep rifts. Musk repeated his grievance that OpenAI, which he cofounded as a nonprofit, became an $800 billion, closed-source, for-profit company. He also renewed his long-standing criticisms of fellow founder Sam Altman, with whom he has spent much of the past two years locked in a legal battle.

sentences.en: Getting there would mean papering over some deep rifts.
sentences.zh: 要达到这一目标就意味着要掩盖一些深刻的裂痕。

sentences.en: Musk repeated his grievance that OpenAI, which he cofounded as a nonprofit, became an $800 billion, closed-source, for-profit company.
sentences.zh: 马斯克重申了他对 OpenAI 的不满，他与他人共同创立的非营利组织 OpenAI 变成了一家价值 8000 亿美元的闭源营利性公司。

sentences.en: He also renewed his long-standing criticisms of fellow founder Sam Altman, with whom he has spent much of the past two years locked in a legal battle.
sentences.zh: 他还重申了对创始人萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 的长期批评，过去两年的大部分时间里，他都与他陷入了法律纠纷。

blocks.html_en: He praised Anthropic's Dario Amodei, who left OpenAI to found the company, as "a very principled person."

sentences.en: He praised Anthropic's Dario Amodei, who left OpenAI to found the company, as "a very principled person."
sentences.zh: 他称赞 Anthropic 的达里奥·阿莫迪 (Dario Amodei) 是“一个非常有原则的人”，他离开了 OpenAI 创建了该公司。

blocks.html_en: If it comes to it, he said, they'll "set aside our personal differences for the good of the world."

sentences.en: If it comes to it, he said, they'll "set aside our personal differences for the good of the world."
sentences.zh: 他说，如果事情真的发生了，他们将“为了世界的利益而抛开我们的个人分歧”。

blocks.html_en: 5. He continues to insist that 'zero' people died from USAID cuts

sentences.en: He continues to insist that 'zero' people died from USAID cuts
sentences.zh: 他继续坚称美国国际开发署削减开支导致“零”人死亡

blocks.html_en: Musk flatly denied that the aid cuts presided over by his short-lived Department of Government Efficiency cost lives. Asked whether children in Africa died because funding was pulled so suddenly, he was emphatic: "Zero," later repeating "0.0"

sentences.en: Musk flatly denied that the aid cuts presided over by his short-lived Department of Government Efficiency cost lives.
sentences.zh: 马斯克断然否认他的短命政府效率部主持的援助削减导致了生命损失。

sentences.en: Asked whether children in Africa died because funding was pulled so suddenly, he was emphatic: "Zero," later repeating "0.0"
sentences.zh: 当被问及非洲儿童是否因为突然停止资金而死亡时，他强调：“零”，后来重复“0.0”

blocks.html_en: He argued that philanthropic organizations like the Gates Foundation and MacKenzie Scott's Yield Giving could have stepped in — and if they didn't, "they're equally responsible" — and dismissed reports of deaths as being from "fraudulent organizations" seeking restored funding.

sentences.en: He argued that philanthropic organizations like the Gates Foundation and MacKenzie Scott's Yield Giving could have stepped in — and if they didn't, "they're equally responsible" — and dismissed reports of deaths as being from "fraudulent organizations" seeking restored funding.
sentences.zh: 他认为，盖茨基金会和麦肯齐·斯科特的收益捐赠等慈善组织本可以介入——如果他们不这样做，“他们同样负有责任”——并驳斥了有关死亡的报道，称其是来自寻求恢复资金的“欺诈组织”。

blocks.html_en: Minton Beddoes pushed back, saying the pace of the cuts "will almost certainly have caused unnecessary suffering and deaths."

blocks.html_en: • Elon Musk

sentences.en: Elon Musk
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克

blocks.html_en: • AI
• Technology

sentences.en: Technology
sentences.zh: 技术

blocks.html_en: • More
• Business

sentences.en: Business
sentences.zh: 商业

content_html_zh:
周四，埃隆·马斯克与《经济学人》主编赞尼·明顿·贝多斯 (Zanny Minton Beddoes) 进行了一次内容广泛的采访，采访内容涵盖了从超级人工智能到他在政府中备受争议的任期等方方面面。

马斯克是世界首富，是特斯拉和 SpaceX 的首席执行官，并拥有社交媒体平台 X，在该平台上他拥有大约 2.5 亿粉丝。 他还经营着自己的人工智能初创公司 xAI，这使他处于他详细讨论的人工智能竞赛的中心。

以下是 90 分钟采访的五个最大收获：

1. 马斯克认为人工智能将在大约5年内超越人类智力的总和

马斯克表示，人工智能可能会在“大约五年内”超越人类的集体智慧，并补充说，很快，“除了人类之外，或许真的没有什么事情是人工智能不能比人类做得更好的了。”

他描绘了未来的乌托邦图景：一个“令人惊叹的丰富时代”，“任何人都可以拥有他们能想到的任何东西”。

然而，对于谁将负责，他不太放心，并将即将到来的智力差距与人类和黑猩猩之间的差距进行比较：“很难想象黑猩猩会负责。”

@elonmusk 说：“也许，除了人类之外，人工智能真的没有什么事情不能比人类做得更好。”他告诉 @zannymb，人工智能可能会在大约五年内超过人类智能的总和。 伦敦时间下午 6 点收看 The Insider：…… pic.twitter.com/Rnw3ngZy3F——《经济学人》(@TheEconomist) 2026 年 7 月 23 日

2. 他已经接受了无法阻止的人工智能风险

马斯克此前曾将人工智能消灭人类的可能性定为 10% 到 20%，但他并没有改变这一说法。 改变的是他的态度：他说他已经得出了一个“哲学结论”，要看到光明的一面，因为人工智能和机器人背后的势头现在是不可阻挡的。

“让我们享受这段旅程，这是我目前的理念，”他说，并暗示即使存在停止按钮，“我们可能也不应该按下它。”

他还讽刺地表示，他自己安全引导人工智能的尝试，包括共同创立 OpenAI（Anthropic 后来将其剥离出来），大部分都加速了这项技术的发展。 他的结论是：“你不能打败他们吗？ 加入他们吧。”

3. 他承认自己对政治“着迷”

“坦白说，我认为我对政治的参与有点太多了，有点得意忘形了，”马斯克在采访中一个更具反思性的时刻说道。

马斯克为特朗普 2024 年连任竞选活动提供了大量捐款，并在政府执政的最初几个月发挥了重要作用，领导了政府效率部。 后来他因总统的“美丽大法案”而与特朗普闹翻。

在采访中，马斯克捍卫了他的 DOGE 工作的根本目标：解决巨额赤字，他说，债务利息支付现在超过了美国国防和情报支出的总和。

他认为，其目标是确保资金不会“浪费或用于欺诈活动”，尽管他表示自己因此“受到了很多批评”。

4. 他希望竞争对手的人工智能实验室能够互相监督，尽管存在私人恩怨

马斯克提议，领先的人工智能公司每隔几周举行一次安全电话会议，并在发布前审查彼此的新模型，他认为竞争对手比政府更有能力发现问题。

明顿·贝多斯 (Minton Beddoes) 表示，她不断听说系统需要在六个月内到位。 马斯克表示，考虑到突破的速度，“六个月是一段很长的时间”。

要达到这一目标就意味着要掩盖一些深刻的裂痕。 马斯克重申了他对 OpenAI 的不满，他与他人共同创立的非营利组织 OpenAI 变成了一家价值 8000 亿美元的闭源营利性公司。 他还重申了对创始人萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 的长期批评，过去两年的大部分时间里，他都与他陷入了法律纠纷。

他称赞 Anthropic 的达里奥·阿莫迪 (Dario Amodei) 是“一个非常有原则的人”，他离开了 OpenAI 创建了该公司。

他说，如果事情真的发生了，他们将“为了世界的利益而抛开我们的个人分歧”。

5. 他继续坚称美国国际开发署削减开支导致“零”人死亡

马斯克断然否认他的短命政府效率部主持的援助削减导致了生命损失。 当被问及非洲儿童是否因为突然停止资金而死亡时，他强调：“零”，后来重复“0.0”

他认为，盖茨基金会和麦肯齐·斯科特的收益捐赠等慈善组织本可以介入——如果他们不这样做，“他们同样负有责任”——并驳斥了有关死亡的报道，称其是来自寻求恢复资金的“欺诈组织”。

明顿·贝多斯反驳说，削减开支的速度“几乎肯定会造成不必要的痛苦和死亡”。

在这里观看完整的交流：

.@elonmusk 表示，DOGE 对美国国际开发署的削减导致零人死亡。 用这位 SpaceX 和特斯拉的亿万富翁首席执行官的话来说，去年他担任美国政府效率部部长时，帮助向伐木工提供了这项政策。 在独家采访中，@zannymb... pic.twitter.com/BilkbWrJHF—《经济学人》(@TheEconomist) 2026 年 7 月 23 日

Georgia 是《商业内幕》伦敦办事处的研究员。在加入《商业内幕》之前，她曾在日本最大的报纸《读卖新闻》工作，并在《金融时报》实习。 她是一名获得 NCTJ 资格的记者，拥有伯明翰大学哲学学位。 您可以通过电子邮件 ghennessy@insider.com 与她联系
